FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Morgan Stanley wins digital asset trust charter approval, cannabis banking reform resurfaces, and SCOTUS affirms Fed independence while Trump mulls removal powers.

bullets.text: Morgan Stanley gets OCC conditional approval for digital-asset trust charter, pending $50M tier-1 capital maintenance and regulator nonobjection on business changes.
Sen. Merkley reintroduces cannabis banking reform legislation, timing opportune after DEA rescheduling of medical cannabis opens regulatory pathway for bank participation.
Supreme Court rules 5-4 Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook, affirming central bank independence while expanding presidential removal powers in other contexts.
Capital One positioning itself as stable corporate banking partner in Texas amid accelerating regional bank consolidation and market share competition.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Wealth consolidation intensifying: major RIA roll-up, platform convergence, and regulatory tailwinds for retirement income solutions.

bullets.text: EP Wealth snaps up Opes for $900M as RIA consolidation continues; adding cash incentives for non-shareholder staff signals fierce talent competition in roll-ups.
Autocallable strategies moving into ETF wrappers via Calamos—structured income demand validating the shift to liquid, tax-efficient product formats.
Regulatory guardrails falling on retirement income/decumulation strategies in DC plans, clearing a lane for advisors to embed these offerings.
Hightower president exits after 2.5 months citing personal reasons—leadership churn amid broader consolidation wave.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Proxy advisers notch another legal win against ESG rules, data center boom creates major underwriting opportunity, and Florida property insurance shows mixed signals.

bullets.text: Proxy advisers ISS and Glass Lewis win third legal challenge against Republican-backed ESG restrictions; federal judge blocked Indiana's disclosure law, protecting their business model from state-level regulatory squeeze.
Hyperscale data center capex to exceed $300B annually by 2027—major new underwriting opportunity across P&C, cyber, and specialty lines as AI infrastructure buildout accelerates.
LA Palisades wildfire arson trial ends in mistrial; jury deadlocked on liability for costliest wildfire on record, leaving insurer exposure unresolved.
Carbon Underwriting (Lloyd's delegated-authority specialist) secures growth equity from FTV Capital to scale underwriting and platform operations.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mixed fintech fortunes: Philippine mobile payments surge toward mega-IPO while UK digital banking stalls; structural shifts ripple across capital markets and payments infrastructure.

bullets.text: Mynt targeting $1.5bn IPO for GCash—Philippines' largest fintech listing ever, riding dominant mobile wallet position in cash-heavy, underbanked market.
Atom Bank sale collapsing after failing to attract bidders at £600M ask; once-hyped UK digital challenger now facing reality check on valuation.
LSE modeling worst-case scenario of 200+ UK-listed companies migrating to US exchanges, exposing competitive vulnerability in regulatory environment and market infrastructure.
Non-listed BDCs creating headwinds in private debt secondaries fundraising as capital flows compete; secondary market growth expectations facing pressure.

PE & Deals.lede: LP skepticism meets deal momentum: Colorado PERA cuts PE exposure amid underperformance, while Triton eyes €470m carveout and BCI triples 2025 deployment.

bullets.text: $75bn Colorado PERA reconsidering PE allocation after sustained underperformance versus benchmarks—signals broader LP dissatisfaction with returns.
Triton in advanced talks for Bureau Veritas fuel testing/inspection business (€470m) as PE capital targets regulated, cash-generative subsectors.
BCI tripled 2025 PE outlays under new global head Jon Salon, betting on 'complex' opportunities as LP dry powder persists despite performance concerns.
European PE defence mega-fund facing political headwinds on foreign ownership and strategic-asset control, complicating deal structuring in the sector.

Markets & Macro.lede: Digital banking M&A stalls, Supreme Court protects Fed independence and deposit insurance, while deleveraging risks spike and PE LPs flee to debt structures.

bullets.text: Atom Bank's sale process has stalled below its £600m target with no bids meeting expectations, signaling broader compression in digital banking valuations.
Supreme Court upheld FDIC deposit insurance caps in Salazar v. Yates, averting a potential financial crisis from a flood of covered-deposit claims.
Morgan Stanley warns deleveraging risk is rising amid quarter-end funding pressures and tightening financial conditions.
PE LPs committed $9bn to debt-like structures in 2025 (up 50% YoY), fleeing traditional equity calls as market stress mounts.

Newsletters.lede: Structural shifts in asset flows and advisor behavior, from autocallables gaining traction to Bitcoin ETF outflows and advisors going direct on Substack.

bullets.text: Autocallable ETFs hit $2.5B in AUM in year one by finally unlocking a previously opaque structured-products market that even sophisticated advisors couldn't navigate.
Bitcoin ETFs experiencing record outflows as crypto enters a downturn—tracking broader digital-asset sentiment deterioration and redemption patterns.
Longer lifespans could gut the $84 trillion wealth transfer if retirees spend assets on extended care rather than preserve estates—a potential headwind for estate planning and HNW management.
Advisors increasingly bypassing firm channels via Substack to build direct client relationships; retail alternatives access expanding but adoption remains unclear at scale.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 5 stories spanning AI's impact on work structure, historical labor shifts, pharma economics, academic integrity, and crypto market vulnerabilities.

bullets.text: AI will both consolidate and disperse work depending on coordination costs—some functions move in-house, others out, upending traditional outsourcing logic.
Typewriter adoption historically boosted female labor participation but suppressed fertility and marriage rates, a sector-specific technology shock with demographic spillover.
Pharma's current high-fixed-cost, low-marginal-cost model enabled price discrimination (NHS arbitrage); gene therapy and cell therapy may break that economics.
Brown econ professor caught systematic LLM cheating in advanced course; elite-school scale of academic fraud via AI access now visible.

all.lede: Fintech valuations under pressure as Atom Bank's sale stalls, while Morgan Stanley wins digital-asset credentials and emerging market payments heat up.

bullets.text: Atom Bank sale collapsing below £600M target—no bidders meeting shareholder expectations, signaling broader digital banking M&A headwinds.
Morgan Stanley gets OCC conditional approval for digital-asset trust charter, unlocking institutional crypto custody play with $50M tier-1 capital requirement.
Mynt (GCash operator) planning $1.5B Philippines IPO—largest fintech listing ever—riding wave of mobile payments in underbanked market.
